content.text:
The chemical formula for calcium nitrate is **Ca(NO₃)₂**.

## Breaking It Down

- **Calcium (Ca):** Has a +2 charge (Ca²⁺)
- **Nitrate (NO₃):** Has a -1 charge (NO₃⁻)

Because calcium has a +2 charge and each nitrate ion has only a -1 charge, you need **two nitrate ions** to balance one calcium ion. This is why the nitrate group is enclosed in parentheses with a subscript 2.

## Quick Facts

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Formula | Ca(NO₃)₂ |
| Molar mass | ~164.09 g/mol |
| Appearance | White crystalline solid |
| Common uses | Fertilizers, wastewater treatment, concrete additives |

It's also commonly found as a **hydrate**, calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, with the formula **Ca(NO₃)₂·4H₂O**.

Let me know if you'd like more details!
